highlights.description: Design-time threat modeling for proactive security analysis during SDLC
Structured risk assessment with likelihood × impact scoring
Asset inventory with sensitivity classification and compliance mapping
What-if scenario modeling for attack path analysis
Network topology mapping for infrastructure diagrams

added.description: `RiskAssessment` type with likelihood × impact scoring (1-5 scales)
`RiskLevel` categorical risk levels (critical/high/medium/low/info)
`ModelPhase` for SDLC phase tracking (design/development/review/production/incident)
`Asset` type with sensitivity classification and compliance mapping
`SensitivityLevel` for data classification (public/internal/confidential/restricted/secret)
`Scenario` type for what-if attack scenario modeling
`ScenarioType` categorization (external-attack/insider-threat/supply-chain/etc.)
`NetworkInfo` and `CloudInfo` for network topology mapping
`ThreatStatusPotential` for hypothetical threats from design review
`ThreatStatusTheoretical` for threats derived from STRIDE/LINDDUN
Design-time threat modeling guide with SDLC integration
Risk assessment guide with likelihood/impact scales

changed.description: `ThreatModel` extended with Phase, Assets, and Scenarios fields
`ThreatEntry` extended with Risk, LINDDUNCategory, AffectedAssets, AttackVector, Preconditions
`Element` extended with Network and AssetIDs fields

documentation.description: MkDocs Guides section added to navigation

highlights.description: Versioned specification structure following OpenAPI pattern (`versions/v0.4.0/`)
Comprehensive security lifecycle: mitigations, detections, and response actions
Attack tree diagrams with AND/OR logic gates
LINDDUN privacy threat framework with D2 rendering
Control framework mappings: NIST CSF, CIS Controls v8, ISO 27001
Compliance framework references: SOC 2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP

added.description: Security lifecycle types: `Assumption`, `AttackTree`, `Compliance`, `Controls`, `Detection`, `LINDDUN`, `Mitigation`, `ThreatActor`
Attack tree diagram type with AND/OR logic gates (`attack-tree`)
LINDDUN privacy threat framework with 7 categories (L, I, N, D, Di, U, Nc)
Mitigations tracking with status lifecycle (planned, implemented, partial, accepted, transferred)
Threat status tracking with `ThreatEntry` and `ThreatStatus` types
Control framework mappings: NIST CSF, CIS Controls v8, ISO 27001
Compliance framework references: SOC 2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA, FedRAMP, NIST SP 800-53
Threat actor profiles with sophistication, motivation, and resources
Detection capabilities with coverage levels and data sources
Response actions for incident response workflows
Assumptions and prerequisites for documenting model constraints
CVE references in mappings (`CVEMapping` type)
D2 legend rendering for LINDDUN threats and mitigation status
Attack tree D2 rendering with AND/OR gate visualization
STIX 2.1 export for CVE, OWASP, and mitigations
Version parameter for genschema command
Versioned specification structure (`versions/v0.4.0/`)

changed.description: `Mappings` struct extended with CVE, LINDDUN, Controls, and Compliance fields
`DiagramIR` extended with AttackTree, Threats, Mitigations, Detections, ResponseActions
`Legend` extended with ShowLINDDUN and ShowMitigations options
JSON schemas regenerated to include all new types

documentation.description: README updated with new framework tables and usage examples

highlights.description: Agent specifications and Claude Code plugin for threat model diagram creation
Examples moved to separate repository for focused maintenance

added.description: Agent specifications for threat model visualization (attack-flow-visualizer, diagram-quality-reviewer, dfd-creator)
Claude Code plugin with agents, commands, and skills

changed.description: Examples directory removed (moved to separate repository)

dependencies.description: Update GitHub Actions workflows to v0.4.0

highlights.description: ThreatModel canonical format for multi-diagram threat models with shared metadata and framework mappings
JSON Schema generation and embedding for validation support

added.description: `ThreatModel` type as canonical format for multi-diagram threat models with shared metadata
`schema/` package with JSON Schema generation and embedding (`threat-model.schema.json`, `diagram.schema.json`)
`cmd/genschema` tool for generating JSON schemas from Go types
CLI auto-detection of ThreatModel vs DiagramIR format
CLI generates separate D2 files for each diagram when processing ThreatModel
STIX 2.1 export for OpenClaw example

changed.description: CLI `validate` command reports ThreatModel format and diagram count
Consolidated STIX exports into single vulnerability file

documentation.description: Architecture diagram added to README showing ThreatModel flow
README updated for ThreatModel as recommended format
MkDocs documentation updated for ThreatModel format

highlights.description: JSON-based intermediate representation for threat modeling diagrams with D2 and STIX 2.1 export
Comprehensive security framework mappings: MITRE ATT&CK, MITRE ATLAS, OWASP Top 10 (API/LLM/Web), CWE, CVSS, STRIDE

added.description: JSON IR schema for threat modeling diagrams (DFD, Attack Chain, Sequence)
`ir` package with types for elements, boundaries, flows, attacks, actors, and messages
`ir/mappings.go` with framework mapping types for MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, OWASP, CWE, CVSS, and STRIDE
`diagram` package for programmatic diagram construction with D2 rendering
`stix` package for STIX 2.1 export (Bundle, AttackPattern, ThreatActor, Infrastructure, Indicator, Vulnerability)
`stride` package with STRIDE threat category types and color mappings
`killchain` package with MITRE ATT&CK tactics and Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain phases
`tms` CLI with `generate`, `validate`, and `version` subcommands
`tms generate --stix` flag for STIX 2.1 JSON export
`tms generate --svg` flag to render D2 diagrams to SVG
D2 style library with classes for STRIDE threats, trust boundaries, DFD elements, and attack flows
OpenClaw WebSocket vulnerability example with DFD, attack chain, and sequence diagrams
Demo vulnerable server and malicious page for educational attack demonstration
Validation for diagram type-specific field requirements

changed.description: CLI migrated from `flag` to Cobra framework for consistent experience and extensibility

build.description: GitHub Actions workflows for CI, lint, and CodeQL SAST
Dependabot configuration for Go modules and GitHub Actions
